commonIssues: Root rot from overwatering — better kept too dry than too wet (Penn State); brown or nonexistent roots indicate excess moisture
Yellowing or pale/scorched leaves from too much direct sun; grow in bright indirect or filtered light
Loss of variegation in low light (variegated cultivars)
Mealybugs, scale insects, and spider mites; treat with insecticidal soap
Leaf tip/margin blackening from over- or underwatering or excess fertilizer
All parts mildly toxic if ingested (calcium oxalate) — keep away from children and pets
Light values represent bright indirect light, not direct sun; avoid prolonged direct sunlight
Provide a moss pole or trellis if larger climbing foliage is desired; otherwise trails well from hanging baskets

source.citation: Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC, 'How to Grow Pothos Indoors (Epipremnum spp.)': allow soil to dry completely before watering; airy, well-draining soil mix; root rot from overwatering
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC: temperatures 60-70F nights and 70-85F days (15.5-29.5C); avoid AC/heat vents and cold windowsills
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC: fertilize with houseplant fertilizer every other month (~60 days) during spring and summer; bright indirect light, tolerates low light

University of Wisconsin-Madison Horticulture Extension, 'Pothos, Epipremnum aureum': filtered light, does not tolerate much direct sunlight; 70-90F (heat stress used ~32C); fertilize every other month except winter; hardy only in zones 10-12

Penn State Extension, 'Pothos as a Houseplant': water when soil is dry, better kept too dry than too wet; 60-80F range; well-draining soilless potting mix; all parts poisonous (non-lethal) to humans and pets

South Dakota State University Extension, 'Pothos (Devil's Ivy): House Plant How-To': tolerates down to ~50F (10C); bright indirect light, low-light areas do fine; bi-monthly balanced fertilizer, none in winter dormancy

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, Epipremnum aureum 'Neon': zones 10-12, part shade / bright indirect light, medium water; peaty potting mix; roots may rot in poorly drained soils; scale, mealybugs, mites; stems to 20 ft

Confidence note: sources say 'water when soil is dry' without a fixed interval — 7 days is a conservative indoor estimate. No extension source specifies pot volume — 2 L minimum (~15 cm / 6-inch pot) is an estimate from standard houseplant practice and MoBot repotting guidance. Light hours are an indoor bright-indirect equivalent, not measured direct-sun hours; extensions describe light qualitatively (bright indirect, tolerates low light).
